evals.prompt: Show me what containers are currently running across all my servers.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend `kamal details` to show details of all containers (proxy, app, and accessory containers) grouped by host. For just the app, should mention `kamal app containers` (show app containers on servers) and `kamal app details` (show details about app containers). May suggest reading config/deploy.yml to confirm which hosts and roles are in play. Should not invent flags or output fields that aren't in the Kamal docs.
assertions: Recommends `kamal details` for all containers across hosts
Mentions `kamal app containers` and/or `kamal app details` for app-only containers
Describes details as grouped by host / per server
Does not invent flags or behaviors not in the Kamal docs

evals.prompt: i need to put the site into maintenance mode while i run a migration, then bring it back
evals.expected_output: Should give `kamal app maintenance` to enter maintenance mode and explain that kamal-proxy intercepts requests and returns 503 responses using a built-in HTML error page. Should mention customizing the page text with `kamal app maintenance --message "..."`. Should mention that custom error pages can be served via the `error_pages_path` config option. Should give `kamal app live` to return the app to live mode afterward.
assertions: Recommends `kamal app maintenance` to enter maintenance mode
Explains kamal-proxy returns 503 responses
Mentions the `--message` option to customize the maintenance message
Mentions `error_pages_path` for custom error pages
Recommends `kamal app live` to return to live mode

evals.prompt: how do i open a rails console on the server?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend `kamal app exec -i 'bin/rails console'` (or `--interactive`), which runs interactively over SSH in a new container made from the most recent app image. Should note the interactive command defaults to the primary host and that `--hosts` connects to another. Should mention `--reuse` to run in the currently running container instead of a fresh one. Should not fabricate other flags.
assertions: Recommends `kamal app exec -i` / `--interactive` with `bin/rails console`
Notes the interactive session defaults to the primary host
Mentions `--hosts` to target a different host
Mentions `--reuse` to use the currently running container
Does not invent unsupported flags

evals.prompt: Run `ruby -v` on every server, and then check `.ruby-version` only on the primary.
evals.expected_output: For all servers: `kamal app exec 'ruby -v'`. For the primary only: `kamal app exec --primary 'cat .ruby-version'` or the short form `kamal app exec -p 'cat .ruby-version'`. Should explain `kamal app exec` runs the command inside the app container on the servers, and that `--primary`/`-p` restricts it to the primary host.
assertions: Uses `kamal app exec 'ruby -v'` to run on all servers
Uses `kamal app exec --primary` or `-p` for the primary-only command
Explains exec runs inside the app container on the servers
Correctly maps `-p` to `--primary`

evals.prompt: who deployed last and when? i want to see the history of what's been run on the boxes
evals.expected_output: Should recommend `kamal audit`, which shows the latest commands run on each server. Should explain it tails `.kamal/app-audit.log` on each host and returns a per-server timeline with timestamped events such as pushed env files, pulled image, booted app version, tagging the latest image, and pruned containers/images. Should not claim audit shows information beyond what is in that log.
assertions: Recommends `kamal audit`
Explains it shows the latest commands run on each server
Mentions the per-server `.kamal/app-audit.log` timeline
References grounded event examples (e.g. booted app version, pulled image, pruned containers)

evals.prompt: What's the difference between `kamal app version` and `kamal version`?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that `kamal app version` shows the app version currently running on the servers (the deployed app), while `kamal version` returns the version of the Kamal CLI you have installed (e.g. 2.12.0). Should make clear these answer different questions and should not conflate them.
assertions: `kamal app version` = app version currently running on servers
`kamal version` = installed Kamal CLI version (e.g. 2.12.0)
Clearly distinguishes the deployed app version from the Kamal CLI version

evals.prompt: Before I run a command I want to double-check the config Kamal will actually use â€” roles, hosts, and the image it'll deploy. How?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend `kamal config`, which displays the resolved config including roles, hosts, primary_host, version, repository, absolute_image, service_with_version, and builder/accessories/logging settings. Should frame it as confirming what a command will target before running it. Should not invent config keys beyond those shown in the Kamal config output.
assertions: Recommends `kamal config`
Mentions it shows resolved roles, hosts, and the image/version to deploy (e.g. absolute_image)
Frames it as verifying targets before running a command
Does not invent config keys not present in the Kamal docs
